Sigma0 (x) = RotR7(x)  xor RotR18(x) xor ShR3(x)
Sigma1 (x) = RotR17(x) xor RotR19(x) xor ShR10(x)
w[i] = Sigma1(w[i-2]) + w[i-7] + Sigma0(w[i-15]) + w[i-16]

$\sigma_0(x) = \text{ROTR}^7(x) \oplus \text{ROTR}^{18}(x) \oplus \text{SHR}^3(x)$

1. Let’s convert “projectfpga.com” to binary:

01110000 01110010 01101111 01101010 01100101
01100011 01110100 01100110 01110000 01100111
01100001 00101110 01100011 01101111 01101101

2. Add 1 to the end of the data:

01110000 01110010 01101111 01101010 01100101
01100011 01110100 01100110 01110000 01100111
01100001 00101110 01100011 01101111 01101101
1

3. Fill in with zeros until the data becomes a multiple of 512 without the last 64 bits (in our case 448 bits):

01110000 01110010 01101111 01101010 01100101 01100011 01110100 01100110
01110000 01100111 01100001 00101110 01100011 01101111 01101101 10000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000

4. Add 64 bits to the end, where 64 bits is a big-endian integer denoting the length of the input data in binary. In our case 120, in binary — “1111000”.

01110000 01110010 01101111 01101010 01100101 01100011 01110100 01100110
01110000 01100111 01100001 00101110 01100011 01101111 01101101 10000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000
00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 01111000

1. Copy the input from step 1 into a new array, where each record is a 32-bit word:

01110000011100100110111101101010 01100101011000110111010001100110
01110000011001110110000100101110 01100011011011110110110110000000
00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000001111000

2. Add 48 more words, initialized to zero, to get an array w[0…63]:

0   01110000011100100110111101101010 01100101011000110111010001100110
2   01110000011001110110000100101110 01100011011011110110110110000000
4   00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
6   00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
8   00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
10  00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
12  00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
14  00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000001111000
16  00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
18  00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
20  00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
22  00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000
...
...
62  00000000000000000000000000000000 00000000000000000000000000000000

Converting to HEX

Word [0..15] (32-bit, big-endian)
Word [0]   70 72 6f 6a 65 63 74 66
Word [2]   70 67 61 2e 63 6f 6d 80
Word [4]   00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
Word [6]   00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
Word [8]   00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
Word [10]  00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
Word [12]  00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
Word [14]  00 00 00 00 00 00 00 78

Generate W[16..63]
Sigma0 (x) = RotR7(x)  xor RotR18(x) xor ShR3(x)
Sigma1 (x) = RotR17(x) xor RotR19(x) xor ShR10(x)
w[i] = Sigma1(w[i-2]) + w[i-7] + Sigma0(w[i-15]) + w[i-16]

Calculate W[16]
W[16] = Sigma1(W[14]) + W[9] + Sigma0(W[1]) + W[0]

RotR7  (0x65637466) => 01100101011000110111010001100110 = 1100 1100 1100 1010 1100 0110 1110 1000 (0xCCCAC6E8)
RotR18 (0x65637466) => 01100101011000110111010001100110 = 1101 1101 0001 1001 1001 1001 0101 1000 (0xDD199958)
ShR3   (0x65637466) => 01100101011000110111010001100110 = 0000 1100 1010 1100 0110 1110 1000 1100 (0x0CAC6E8C)
Sigma0(W[1])                                            = 0001 1101 0111 1111 0011 0001 0011 1100 (0x1D7F313C)
Sigma0(W[1])  = 0x1D7F313C
Sigma1(W[14]) = 0x00000000
W[9]          = 0x00000000
W[0]          = 0x70726f6a

W[16]         = 0x8DF1A0A6

W[17] = Sigma1(W[15]) + W[10] + Sigma0(W[2]) + W[1],
where W[15] = 00000078, W[2] = 7067612e, W[1] = 65637466
Sigma0  = 0x8aa7befe
Sigma1  = 0x00330000
W[17]   = 0xf03e3364

W[18] = Sigma1(W[16]) + W[11] + Sigma0(W[3]) + W[2]

W[63] = Sigma1(W[61]) + W[56] + Sigma0(W[48]) + W[47]

### Compression cycle
a = h0 = 6a09e667
b = h1 = bb67ae85

d = h3 = a54ff53a

Sigma_S0(x) = RotR2(x) xor RotR13(x) xor RotR22(x)
Sigma_S1(x) = RotR6(x) xor RotR11(x) xor RotR25(x)

T1 = h + Sigma_S1(e) + Ch(e,f,g) + K[t] + W[t]
T2 = Sigma_S0(a) + Maj(a,b,c)
